extra.reasoning: The property is monotonicity in hemoptysis: setting hemoptysis=true while holding all other inputs fixed must never lower acuity. Examining all eight rules (r1-r8), none of them reference hemoptysis in their conditions, and hemoptysis appears in no rule action. The acuity output is computed solely from the other eight inputs (fever, chest_pain, stiff_neck, palpitations, back_pain, abdominal_pain, dyspnea, confusion) via the priority-resolved rules plus the routine default. Because hemoptysis is a completely non-functional input with respect to acuity, flipping it from false to true leaves acuity exactly unchanged for every possible input assignment. An unchanged (equal) acuity trivially satisfies the requirement of being "no lower than before." Hence no input can witness a decrease in acuity due to setting hemoptysis true, and the property holds over the entire input space.
